flags.stage_neg1_fallback: stage_neg1 requested but pivot budget exhausted (NEG1_PIVOT_BUDGET=3): Case 6b (Stage 0.5 reject fast-path) + regression (a) — the reviewer's reject is conjecture-level: both headline results refute internal in-repo conjectures rather than a named published estimator/workflow, so the artifact cannot clear novelty_target=flagship under the negative-result rules. This is not a derivation slip a Stage-0 redo can fix on the same .tex; the conjecture choice itself is the defect.

proposed_from.topic: Spectral phase transitions for staggered two-way fixed effects on Markov-modulated treatment adoption: a mixing-time/spectral-gap threshold theorem that sharply separates the point-identified, partially-identified, and non-identified regimes for the dynamic ATT, together with the matching FWL/minimal-basis structural change on each side of the threshold. Goal: a flagship-tier identification phase-transition result that ties the spectral gap of the adoption Markov chain to identifiability of dynamic treatment effects, generalizing static minimal-basis theory and providing a Lean-formalizable identification frontier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-repo results remain AutoID/Panel/Regression/PartialID.lean:PartialBounds for the row-space/fiber trichotomy and AutoID/Panel/Theorems/Q1_MinimalBasis.lean:T4_local_minimality for the minimal-basis language. The proposal’s gap is the Markov valid-origin clean-window singular-value threshold for TWFE/FWL event-window rows, not generic finite-dimensional partial identification or static Q1 basis minimality.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are sunabraham2021eventstudy on event-study contamination, hu2022switchback on Markov mixing rates, schmidheiny2023event on event-study/distributed-lag equivalence with multiple events, and borusyak2024eventstudy on event-study support/imputation. None states the proposed valid-origin Markov clean-window singular-value phase transition separating regular point identification, retained-row partial identification, and nonregular visibility for causal switch-on ATT coordinates."}
seed_list: Prior work athey2022design would predict that randomized adoption timing gives a weighted causal estimand; our seed conjectures that Markov-modulated randomized timing has a spectral bottleneck where the FWL history Gram matrix becomes singular and the dynamic ATT becomes only partially identified.

Prior work sunabraham2021eventstudy establishes contamination of event-study leads/lags under heterogeneous effects; our seed conjectures that Markov persistence creates a sharper rank-collapse phenomenon where some relative-period contrasts cease to be estimable at all.

Prior work bojinov2021panel shows serial correlation in assignments can make fixed-effect estimands causally uninterpretable; our seed conjectures an exact spectral-gap threshold separating interpretable, partially interpretable, and non-interpretable dynamic ATT functionals.

Prior work borusyak2024eventstudy establishes robust imputation when untreated rows span target predictions; our seed conjectures that Markov switch scarcity changes the row span discontinuously, causing an imputation-to-bounds transition for switch-on ATT.

Prior work rambachan2023parallel partially identifies DiD targets by relaxing parallel trends; our seed conjectures partial identification even under exact untreated TWFE baseline, caused solely by a Markov spectral support bottleneck.

Prior work dechaisemartin2020twfe emphasizes negative TWFE weights; our seed conjectures that a design can have the relevant sign diagnostics under control while still losing dynamic ATT identification through a vanishing Gram eigenvalue.

Prior work goodmanbacon2021timing decomposes staggered TWFE into pairwise comparisons; our seed conjectures that Markov-modulated adoption can leave all pairwise comparisons formally present while their span is too low-dimensional for the dynamic ATT profile.

Prior work callaway2021multiple identifies group-time ATT components under staggered DiD assumptions; our seed conjectures that the analogous Markov history-time ATT lattice has a visible-row-space frontier that must be reported before aggregation.

Prior work athey2021matrix treats time-dependent missingness in causal panel matrix completion; our seed conjectures a causal-FWL counterpart where Markov missingness creates a spectral phase transition between hard identification and regularized extrapolation.

Prior work roth2023rollout derives efficient estimators for as-good-as-random rollout timing; our seed conjectures that slow Markov rollout mixing separates efficiency from identification because some dynamic ATT directions have no growing information.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work athey2022design would predict that randomized adoption timing gives a weighted causal estimand; our seed conjectures that Markov-modulated randomized timing has a spectral bottleneck where the FWL history Gram matrix becomes singular and the dynamic ATT becomes only partially identified.

anchor_paper.author: Athey and Imbens
anchor_paper.claim: Under random assignment of adoption dates, standard DiD is unbiased for a particular weighted average causal effect.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process variation; not blocked because the proposed mechanism is a rank/support phase transition, not merely Bernoulli-to-Markov extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Athey and Imbens because they already analyze randomized adoption timing.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their adoption-date design is not a Markov history Gram-rank frontier and does not classify partial/non-identification regimes for dynamic switch-on ATT.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work sunabraham2021eventstudy establishes contamination of event-study leads/lags under heterogeneous effects; our seed conjectures that Markov persistence creates a sharper rank-collapse phenomenon where some relative-period contrasts cease to be estimable at all.

anchor_paper.author: Sun and Abraham
anchor_paper.claim: TWFE event-study coefficients can be contaminated by effects from other relative periods under variation in treatment timing.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because the new claim is loss of estimable directions, not another contamination decomposition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Sun and Abraham because contamination already explains bad dynamic coefficients.
seed_details.rebuttal: Contamination at fixed support does not give a spectral row-rank trichotomy or a partial-identification frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work bojinov2021panel shows serial correlation in assignments can make fixed-effect estimands causally uninterpretable; our seed conjectures an exact spectral-gap threshold separating interpretable, partially interpretable, and non-interpretable dynamic ATT functionals.

anchor_paper.author: Bojinov, Rambachan, and Shephard
anchor_paper.claim: Common linear fixed-effect estimators may not recover a causally interpretable estimand with dynamic effects and serially correlated assignments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Settled-but-unclear scope; not blocked because the seed proposes a sharp threshold for the failure mode.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Bojinov et al. because they already discuss serial correlation and fixed effects.
seed_details.rebuttal: They do not derive a Markov spectral-gap row-space threshold for Q1 minimal-basis TWFE identification.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work borusyak2024eventstudy establishes robust imputation when untreated rows span target predictions; our seed conjectures that Markov switch scarcity changes the row span discontinuously, causing an imputation-to-bounds transition for switch-on ATT.

anchor_paper.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess
anchor_paper.claim: With unrestricted treatment-effect heterogeneity, the efficient robust estimator has an imputation form based on untreated observations.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Identification-estimation split; not blocked because the mechanism is row-span failure revealed by Markov support, not deriving another ATT estimator.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BJS because their row-span condition already says when imputation works.
seed_details.rebuttal: BJS does not characterize the Markov spectral parameter at which the FWL/history row span changes dimension.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work rambachan2023parallel partially identifies DiD targets by relaxing parallel trends; our seed conjectures partial identification even under exact untreated TWFE baseline, caused solely by a Markov spectral support bottleneck.

anchor_paper.author: Rambachan and Roth
anchor_paper.claim: DiD/event-study parameters can be partially identified under restrictions on possible violations of parallel trends.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation; not blocked because the identification gap is caused by design row-space collapse, not by changing ATE to ATT.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Rambachan and Roth because partial identification is their main result.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their partiality comes from trend-deviation restrictions; here it comes from exact-baseline Markov support/rank loss.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work dechaisemartin2020twfe emphasizes negative TWFE weights; our seed conjectures that a design can have the relevant sign diagnostics under control while still losing dynamic ATT identification through a vanishing Gram eigenvalue.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille
anchor_paper.claim: TWFE coefficients are weighted sums of group-period ATEs and some weights may be negative.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; it replaces sign pathology with rank pathology.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from negative-weight decompositions because bad signs already diagnose TWFE failure.
seed_details.rebuttal: The repository already shows sign is not locked to the eigenvalue; the proposed novelty is identification rank, not sign.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work goodmanbacon2021timing decomposes staggered TWFE into pairwise comparisons; our seed conjectures that Markov-modulated adoption can leave all pairwise comparisons formally present while their span is too low-dimensional for the dynamic ATT profile.

anchor_paper.author: Goodman-Bacon
anchor_paper.claim: TWFE DiD with variation in timing is a weighted average of all possible two-group/two-period DiD estimators.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition; not blocked because the surprising mechanism is comparison-span deficiency despite positive comparison presence.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Goodman-Bacon because the decomposition already lists all comparisons and weights.
seed_details.rebuttal: A comparison list is not a rank theorem for dynamic history ATT basis directions under Markov support collapse.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work callaway2021multiple identifies group-time ATT components under staggered DiD assumptions; our seed conjectures that the analogous Markov history-time ATT lattice has a visible-row-space frontier that must be reported before aggregation.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Group-time ATT parameters are identified in multi-period DiD with variation in timing under appropriate parallel-trends conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation; not blocked because the new target reveals a visibility/row-space identification gap hidden by group-time aggregation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Callaway and Sant'Anna because unsupported group-time cells are simply not estimated.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposal aims for the exact Markov spectral condition under which support disappears or collapses after FWL residualization.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work athey2021matrix treats time-dependent missingness in causal panel matrix completion; our seed conjectures a causal-FWL counterpart where Markov missingness creates a spectral phase transition between hard identification and regularized extrapolation.

anchor_paper.author: Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, and Khosravi
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Matrix-completion estimators can handle panel counterfactual outcome missingness with time-series dependence.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method M from setting S1 to S2; not blocked because the extension changes hard identification status rather than applying matrix completion to TWFE.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from matrix completion because missingness and regularization are already analyzed.
seed_details.rebuttal: Matrix completion does not yield the Q1 FWL minimal-basis row-space theorem or dynamic ATT phase diagram.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work roth2023rollout derives efficient estimators for as-good-as-random rollout timing; our seed conjectures that slow Markov rollout mixing separates efficiency from identification because some dynamic ATT directions have no growing information.

anchor_paper.author: Roth and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: For staggered rollout designs with as-good-as-random timing, an efficient estimator weakly dominates existing approaches.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process variation; not blocked because the mechanism is no-growing-information rank loss, not another efficient estimator.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from rollout efficiency theory because poor overlap just reduces precision.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed draws a boundary where precision loss becomes partial or non-identification in the limiting FWL moment system.

proposed_from.literature_map: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille 2020, AER, dechaisemartin2020twfe: TWFE estimates weighted sums with possible negative weights; tension: sign pathologies do not by themselves describe rank/support loss. Goodman-Bacon 2021, Journal of Econometrics, goodmanbacon2021timing: staggered TWFE decomposes into 2x2 comparisons; open angle: comparison existence is not the same as full dynamic-history row rank. Sun and Abraham 2021, Journal of Econometrics, sunabraham2021eventstudy: event-study leads/lags are contaminated by other relative periods; tension: contamination is studied at fixed support, not under Markov support collapse. Callaway and Sant'Anna 2021, Journal of Econometrics, callaway2021multiple: group-time ATT parameters are identified under staggered DiD assumptions; open angle: which dynamic history-time ATT components remain visible under persistent Markov treatment. Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess 2024, Review of Economic Studies, borusyak2024eventstudy: imputation event studies require untreated-prediction row span; phenomenon: Markov persistence can move a treated row out of the stable span in the limit. Bojinov, Rambachan, and Shephard 2021, Quantitative Economics, bojinov2021panel: serially correlated assignments break causal interpretation of common FE estimators; open angle: exact spectral threshold for when the FE moment system loses causal content. Athey and Imbens 2022, Journal of Econometrics, athey2022design: design-based staggered adoption under random adoption dates yields weighted ATE interpretation; tension: Markov-modulated adoption is randomized but not date-exchangeable. Roth and Sant'Anna 2023, JPE Microeconomics, roth2023rollout: efficient estimation for as-good-as-random staggered rollouts; open angle: efficiency presumes identifiable target directions. Rambachan and Roth 2023, Review of Economic Studies, rambachan2023parallel: partial identification from relaxed trend assumptions; phenomenon: partiality may arise from assignment-spectrum support even when untreated TWFE baseline is exact. Athey et al. 2021, JASA, athey2021matrix: panel missingness with time-series dependence matters for counterfactual imputation; tension: regularization and hard identification separate when Markov missingness collapses cell support.
